packageRules.description: The deno manager extracts npm: specifiers from deno.json tasks but refuses to update them because it cannot touch deno.lock for task deps, which only emits a warning. Disable that depType here and track the same specifiers via the customManager below, which bumps the version string without a lockfile update (deno re-resolves it into deno.lock on the next task run).

tomat-core-updater depends on `time = 0.3` directly, but the whole Rust workspace shares one Cargo.lock and tauri caps its transitive `time` at <0.3.52. Without this, Renovate keeps trying to bump the shared `time` to 0.3.52+ and the Cargo.lock artifact update fails against tauri's requirement. Cap it until tauri relaxes the bound (then remove this rule).
Combine every dependency update into a single weekly PR titled `fix: update dependencies`. groupName implicitly groups majors in too, so the rule below peels them back out.
Major updates are riskier to batch with routine ones, so route them to their own weekly PR (`fix: update dependencies (major)`) instead of the combined one above.

Keep the Android build toolchain in lockstep. The Kotlin Gradle plugin, AGP, and the androidx/material libraries are version-coupled: androidx artifacts embed Kotlin metadata the Kotlin compiler must be new enough to read (a lib compiled with Kotlin 2.1 fails to compile under the 1.9 compiler). The default major/minor split above routed the Kotlin MAJOR (1.9 -> 2.x) into its own PR while androidx bumped in the combined PR, leaving the compiler too old and breaking the Android release build. This rule (last, so it wins) regroups all of these into one PR regardless of update type, so a Kotlin major always lands with the androidx bumps that need it.

customManagers.description: Some scripts pass npm: specifiers as subprocess args. These are in-source, which the deno manager ignores, so track them here.

npm: specifiers run from deno.json task commands (oxlint, oxfmt). The deno manager picks these up but cannot update them alongside deno.lock, so it is disabled for tasks above and they are tracked here instead. Anchored to `deno run` so the version-pinned allowScripts entries are left alone.
